verses.text: But they constrained him, saying, Abide with us: for it is toward evening, and the day is far spent. And he went in to tarry with them.
And it came to pass, as he sat at meat with them, he took bread, and blessed it, and brake, and gave to them.
And their eyes were opened, and they knew him; and he vanished out of their sight.
And they said one to another, Did not our heart burn within us, while he talked with us by the way, and while he opened to us the scriptures?

blurb: The disciples on the road to Emmaus discovered that their mysterious companion was the risen Christ only when he broke bread with them—the very moment of revelation was also the moment he disappeared from sight. Sometimes our deepest encounters with the divine come not in grand displays but in simple acts of hospitality and shared meals. Their hearts had been burning with recognition long before their eyes were opened, reminding us that spiritual truth often registers in our hearts before our minds can fully grasp it.
